items.title: Path of Exile Starter
items.text:
A practical guide for users and contributors of the Path of Exile Telegram assistant.

What you will find here

This site covers two workflows: using the bot in Telegram and running/developing the project locally.

items.title: Atlas Companion for Exiles
items.text: Track profitable positions, review vendor recipes, and operate your Telegram bot flow from one place.

items.title: Navigation
items.text:
• Quick Start

    Fast setup steps for local launch with Docker and required environment variables.

    Open guide

  • How To Work With The Bot

    User actions in Telegram, skill flow, and developer notes for extending bot behavior.

    Open guide

  • FAQ

    Answers for webhook setup, data source, CI checks, and common project questions.

    Open FAQ

  • Roadmap

    Current feature status for Telegram and Aggregator modules.

    Open roadmap

items.title: Project Links
items.text:
• Repository: path-of-exile-starter
  • Telegram bot: @poe_consultant_bot

items.title: Does Telegram bot work over Webhook?
items.text: Yes. The bot uses Spring webhook integration.

items.title: Which source is used for market prices?
items.text: The project uses poe.ninja as the primary market data source.

items.title: How to run the bot locally?
items.text:
Use helper scripts:

bash tools/scripts/dev/run_app.sh

Stop services with:

bash tools/scripts/dev/stop_app.sh

items.title: Do I need to call Telegram API manually to register webhook?
items.text: No. Webhook subscription is handled by the application startup flow.

items.title: Which database is used?
items.text: MariaDB with Flyway migrations.

items.title: How is project stability controlled?
items.text: GitHub Actions pipelines run checks on pull requests and on main branch.

items.title: Are dependency versions updated automatically?
items.text: Yes. The repository uses Renovate for automated dependency updates.

items.title: Telegram Module
items.text: Feature Status Integration Tests Database + migration Subscribe webhook on startup Save user info in DB Integration with Aggregator Welcome message Main menu (Start, Settings, Feedback) Start -> Skills Start -> Refresh Skills Start -> Blessing Items Settings flows Feedback flows

items.title: Aggregator Module
items.text: Feature Status Unit Tests Database + migration Initial data loading on startup Scheduled updates Integration with poe.ninja Analyzer for skills Analyzer for blessing items Swagger API

items.title: Prerequisites
items.text:
• Java 25
  • Docker + Docker Compose
  • Telegram bot token and webhook URL

items.title: Configure environment
items.text:
Create or update .env in project root and define bot settings:

TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=<your_bot_token>
TELEGRAM_BOT_WEBHOOK=<public_https_webhook_url>

Configuration keys are read by BotConfiguration from environment variables.

items.title: Run locally
items.text:
Start services and Spring apps with the helper script:

bash tools/scripts/dev/run_app.sh

Stop all local services:

bash tools/scripts/dev/stop_app.sh

items.title: Manual Docker run (alternative)
items.text:
docker compose \
  -f tools/docker/docker-compose.yml \
  -f tools/docker/docker-compose.override.yml \
  up -d --quiet-pull spring-webflux-aggregator spring-telegram-webhook

items.title: Next step
items.text: After startup, open the Telegram bot and follow How To Work With The Bot.

items.title: How To Work With The Bot
items.text: This guide describes both user flow in Telegram and developer flow for maintaining the same behavior in code.
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: User flow
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: 1. Open the bot and start dialog
items.text:
• Open @poe_consultant_bot
  • Use /start
  • Bot responds with the main keyboard
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: 2. Use Skills
items.text:
• Tap Skills from the main menu
  • Use arrows (left/right) to paginate items
  • Use refresh button to reload current page
  • Use Link to guide to open this documentation page
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: 3. Use Vendor Recipes
items.text:
• Tap Vendor Recipes
  • Navigate recipes page by page
  • Review ingredients and price values for the selected league
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: 4. Use Settings
items.text:
• Open Settings
  • Choose one of available leagues
  • Bot stores selected league and uses it in next calculations
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: Developer flow
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: Entry points
items.text:
• Webhook controller receives Telegram update payload.
  • Update handler routes message/callback to dedicated services.

Key classes:

  • modules/spring-telegram-webhook/src/main/java/io/starter/controller/WebhookController.java
  • modules/spring-telegram-webhook/src/main/java/io/starter/handler/UpdateHandler.java
  • modules/spring-telegram-webhook/src/main/java/io/starter/service/CallbackAnswerService.java
  • modules/spring-telegram-webhook/src/main/java/io/starter/service/MessageAnswerService.java
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: Where skill guide link is configured
items.text: Constants.Start.SKILLS_GUIDE_LINK is used by CallbackAnswerService for the inline button Link to guide.
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: Add or change callback behavior
items.text:
1. Add a callback state in CallbackState
  2. Handle it in UpdateHandler
  3. Build message/keyboard in CallbackAnswerService or MessageAnswerService
  4. Add/adjust unit tests under modules/spring-telegram-webhook/src/test/java/io/starter/tests/units/handler
path: How To Work With The Bot

items.title: Debug checklist
items.text:
• Verify TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_BOT_WEBHOOK
  • Ensure local services are healthy in Docker
  • Inspect application logs from both modules
  • Confirm callback payload value matches expected CallbackState
path: How To Work With The Bot
